etymology.description: semantic 氵(水) (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 每(每) (měi) -> sea; ocean
etymology.interpretation: The character 海(hǎi, "sea; ocean") combines the semantic component 氵(水) (shuǐ, "water"), representing water or fluidity, with the phonetic component 每(每) (měi), which provides the pronunciation. The meaning of 'sea or ocean' naturally extends from the association with water, emphasizing a large body of it.

examples.chinese: 孩子们喜欢去海边玩耍(孩子們喜歡去海邊玩耍)。
examples.english: Children like to play by the sea.

examples.chinese: 海洋的蓝色令人心旷神怡(海洋的藍色令人心曠神怡)。
examples.pinyin: Hǎiyáng de lánsè lìng rén xīnkuàngshényí.
examples.english: The blue of the ocean is refreshing.

examples.chinese: 他梦想去环游世界的海洋(他夢想去環遊世界的海洋)。
examples.pinyin: Tā mèngxiǎng qù huányóu shìjiè de hǎiyáng.
examples.english: He dreams of traveling around the world's oceans.
